name: Sadia Afrin
title: Senior Lecturer
bio_raw:
Sadia Afrin is an accomplished academic and ongoing PhD scholar in Bangla Language at the University of Dhaka, specializing in Comparative Language and Dialect. She holds both BA and MA degrees from the same institution and currently serves as a Senior Lecturer at the University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh (ULAB). With over Seven years of teaching experience across institutions including Southeast University and Bangladesh Glorious College, she brings deep expertise in Bangla language and literature.
Her scholarly work includes several peer-reviewed publications covering topics such as postcolonial narratives, geopolitics, gender studies, and ecofeminism. In addition to her teaching and research, she actively contributes to academic and administrative development through her involvement in curriculum design, cultural event coordination, and committee roles focused on Outcome-Based Education (OBE) and academic governance.
Sadia is proficient in using a range of educational and research tools, including Zotero, Turnitin, and Microsoft Office, and has participated in numerous national and international workshops on pedagogy, digital learning, and research methodology. Her dedication to academic excellence, research innovation, and student engagement positions her as a dynamic and forward-thinking educator in the field.
stated_interests: Comparative Literature, Comparative Language, Eco-Feminism, Dialectology, Eco-Linguistics, Socio Linguistics, Feminist Linguistics, Historical Criticism.
